content:
{
  "title": "Function Signature Expansion: Contextual Prompting for Enhanced LLM Code Generation",
  "problem": "LLMs often miss nuanced behaviors or underperform in generating code for complex functions when given minimalistic prompts. Improving code generation fidelity and correctness remains challenging, especially for functions with under-specified requirements.",
  "motivation": "As LLMs are increasingly used for code synthesis, prompt engineering becomes crucial to maximize their performance. Existing methods often provide only the function name and signature, neglecting implicit requirements that developers assume. Contextualizing prompts with expanded signatures or auxiliary information may bridge this gap and improve generated code quality.",
  "method": "We propose 'Function Signature Expansion' (FSE), a prompting technique where, given a plain function signature, the prompt is automatically augmented with: (a) explicit input/output type descriptions, (b) natural language paraphrases of intent, and (c) edge case clarifications inferred from docstrings or test cases. A small retriever model identifies relevant auxiliary information, which is inserted into the prompt directly before code generation. The LLM is then tasked with generating the function implementation using this enriched context.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate FSE on subsets of HumanEval and MBPP, comparing against standard signature-only and docstring-only prompts. Metrics will include pass@1 and pass@10 on corresponding unit tests. Analysis will focus on functions with ambiguous requirements to assess the impact of expanded prompting."
}
